name: write_sdf
short_description: write_sdf command generates flat sdf delay files for event simulation
full_help:
write_sdf

Description: 
write_sdf command generates flat sdf delay files for event simulation

Syntax: 
write_sdf  [-process_corner <arg>] [-cell <arg>] [-filter_cell <arg>]
           [-rename_top <arg>] [-force] [-mode <arg>] [-gzip]
           [-multithread <arg>] [-quiet] [-verbose] <file>

Usage: 
  Name               Description
  ------------------------------
  [-process_corner]  Specify process corner for which SDF delays are 
                     required; Values: slow, fast
                     Default: slow
  [-cell]            Root of the design to write, e.g. des.subblk.cpu
                     Default: whole design
  [-filter_cell]     Confine sdf instances to those contained within this 
                     hierarchical cell and to nets which contain at least one
                     pin within this hierarchical cell, e.g. des.subblk.cpu
                     Default: whole design
  [-rename_top]      Replace name of top module with custom name e.g. netlist
                     Default: new top module name
  [-force]           Overwrite existing SDF file
  [-mode]            Specify sta (Static Timing Analysis) or timesim (Timing 
                     Simulation) mode for SDF
                     Default: timesim
  [-gzip]            write gzipped SDF
  [-multithread]     Run in multithread mode with specified number of threads
                     Default: -1
  [-quiet]           Ignore command errors
  [-verbose]         Suspend message limits during command execution
  <file>             File name

Categories: 
FileIO, Simulation, Timing
